data_source: AI-Estimated (Llama 3.3 70B inference from web context — not official statistics)

exporters.notes: Hongqiao, a state-owned enterprise, is the world's largest aluminum producer, accounting for approximately 15% of global production.

exporters.ownership_note: Emirates Global Aluminium (EGA) is a UAE-based company
exporters.notes: EGA operates smelters in Dubai and Abu Dhabi, accounting for nearly 4% of global aluminum production.

exporters.ownership_note: Alcoa Corporation, Novelis Inc., and Logan Aluminum are major US-based aluminum producers
exporters.notes: Alcoa Corporation is one of the top aluminum producers globally, while Novelis Inc. is the largest recycler of aluminum globally.

exporters.ownership_note: Rio Tinto and Alcoa are major Australian aluminum producers
exporters.notes: Rio Tinto operates aluminum smelters in Australia, while Alcoa has a significant presence in the country.

exporters.ownership_note: Alcoa and Rio Tinto have operations in Canada
exporters.notes: Alcoa and Rio Tinto operate aluminum smelters in Canada, contributing to the country's aluminum exports.

exporters.ownership_note: Vale and Alcoa have operations in Brazil
exporters.notes: Vale operates aluminum smelters in Brazil, while Alcoa has a significant presence in the country.

exporters.ownership_note: Rusal and Norilsk Nickel are major Russian aluminum producers
exporters.notes: Rusal is one of the largest aluminum producers globally, while Norilsk Nickel operates aluminum smelters in Russia.

exporters.ownership_note: Hindalco Industries and Nalco are major Indian aluminum producers
exporters.notes: Hindalco Industries is a leading aluminum producer in India, while Nalco operates aluminum smelters in the country.

exporters.ownership_note: Norsk Hydro is a major Norwegian aluminum producer
exporters.notes: Norsk Hydro operates aluminum smelters in Norway, contributing to the country's aluminum exports.

importers.notes: China is the world's largest consumer of aluminum, driven by its growing construction and manufacturing sectors.
The US is a significant importer of aluminum, driven by its automotive and aerospace industries.
Japan is a major importer of aluminum, driven by its electronics and automotive industries.
South Korea is a significant importer of aluminum, driven by its electronics and automotive industries.
Germany is a major importer of aluminum, driven by its automotive and aerospace industries.
